stemHtml: A Commercial Air Transport (CAT) operator requires specialist work (which falls under the scope of the CAT operator’s approval) to be carried out by an outside contractor. The contractor’s approval to do these activities has NOT yet been granted by the competent authority. Is it still possible for the contractor to undertake the work for the operator?

options.a: Yes, provided the contractor obtains individual approvals for their employees undertaking the work, and agrees with the operator to take full responsibility for the work, in writing.
options.b: No, because the overall responsibility for the work rests with the contractor, the contractor must have received their approval.
options.c: No, this work must be done by the operator because it falls under the scope of their approval.
options.d: Yes, under the approval and oversight of the operator as a subcontracted activity.
